heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions, Made for Wembley
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Wembley | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions for Wembley homes — designed around 1930s semis, built in weeks and installed on site in under a week.
intro:
Wembley is a place where homes work hard. Whether you're in a 1930s Metroland semi near Wembley Park, a bay-fronted terrace in Sudbury or a family house within sight of the stadium arch, the story is often the same: a home you love, in a neighbourhood you don't want to leave, that's simply run out of room.

That's exactly where EazyBase comes in. We design and factory-build modular home extensions in Wembley homeowners can plan with confidence — precision-engineered in our Blackburn facility in as little as four weeks, then installed on site in under a week. New kitchens, dining spaces, home offices, playrooms and garden rooms, without months of scaffolding, skips and disruption.

Because your extension is built indoors before it ever arrives, you know what you're getting: the design you approved, the quality you expect, and a timescale measured in days on site rather than seasons.

localAngles.heading: Extending a Metroland semi
localAngles.body: Much of Wembley Park, Sudbury and Preston Road was built in the 1930s Metroland boom, and those semis share a familiar layout: generous gardens, but a cramped rear kitchen and a side return that never quite earns its keep. A single-storey rear extension is the classic answer — opening the back of the house into a wide kitchen-diner that finally matches how families actually live. Because we survey first and manufacture to millimetre tolerances, our modules are designed around your home's exact dimensions, party walls and garden levels before anything is built.

localAngles.heading: Room for every generation
localAngles.body: Wembley is home to many multigenerational households, with parents, grandparents and grown-up children sharing one address. An extension here often isn't a luxury — it's how a family keeps living together comfortably. A ground-floor extension can create a second living room, a quiet space for an older relative, or a proper study for teenagers and home workers. Because installation takes under a week, the household isn't camping around a building site for months — which matters far more when there are three generations under one roof.

localAngles.heading: Planning and Brent Council
localAngles.body: Every extension in Wembley falls under Brent Council's planning jurisdiction. The good news is that permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions within set size limits — though you should always check with Brent Council before starting, as rights vary by property, and some homes near conservation areas or in flats and maisonettes are treated differently. We help you understand which route applies to your home, prepare accurate drawings from our factory specifications, and design within the rules from day one rather than discovering problems later.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works in Wembley
localAngles.body: Wembley's streets weren't designed for eighteen weeks of skips, deliveries and trade vans — especially around the stadium, where event days bring parking restrictions and heavy traffic. A factory-built extension flips the equation. The messy, weather-dependent work happens in our Blackburn facility, and your module arrives largely complete. On-site installation takes under a week, which we plan carefully around access, neighbours and the local events calendar. And as regeneration continues to lift the area around Wembley Park, improving the home you already own is an increasingly sensible way to invest.

faqs.q: How long does a modular extension take for a Wembley home?
faqs.a: Your extension is built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks while your garden stays untouched. Once it's ready, we deliver and install on site in under a week. Compared with a traditional build, you spend far less time living alongside the works — a real advantage in a busy household.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission from Brent Council?
faqs.a: Not always. Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions within set size limits, but rules vary by property — and homes in conservation areas, flats and maisonettes are treated differently. We always recommend confirming with Brent Council before work begins, and we'll guide you through whichever route applies to your home.

faqs.q: What about access and parking on Wembley's streets?
faqs.a: We plan every installation around your street — road width, parking, overhead cables and neighbouring properties are all assessed during the site survey. Near the stadium we also factor in event days and any temporary restrictions, scheduling delivery for a date when access is straightforward. Most installations need only short-term, pre-arranged parking suspensions, which we help organise.

faqs.q: What do Wembley homeowners typically use their extension for?
faqs.a: The most popular choice locally is a rear kitchen-diner that opens a 1930s semi onto the garden. We also build home offices, kids' playrooms, extra living rooms for multigenerational families, and standalone garden rooms. Every module is designed around how your household actually lives, then finished to the specification you sign off before manufacture.
